title: SRPT Stock Earnings - Sarepta Therapeutics Beats EPS, Misses Revenue for Q2 2024
text:
Sarepta Therapeutics reported mixed Q2 2024 earnings results, beating on earnings per share but missing on revenue expectations. The company's earnings beat came despite softer-than-expected top-line growth, reflecting operational leverage and strong cost management.

Missed Revenue Expectations: Company reported revenue of $362.93 million, falling short of the analyst estimate of $396.26 million by 8.41%. This miss reflected slower-than-anticipated ELEVIDYS ramp following the June 2024 label expansion.

Net Product Revenue: Total net product revenue reached $361 million, still representing a strong 51% increase year-over-year. Breakdown: ELEVIDYS: $121.7 million. EXONDYS 51: $129.8 million. VYONDYS 53: $31.6 million. AMONDYS 45: $7.4 million. PMO total: $238.8 million.

ELEVIDYS Ramp: ELEVIDYS received broad FDA approval in June 2024, making it available to over 80% of Duchenne patients. While this expanded the addressable market significantly, the initial uptake was more measured than some investors had anticipated.

Commercial Performance: Early demand signals for the expanded label were robust. Over 1,000 antibody screening kit requests received within weeks of approval. Enrollment forms exceeded internal expectations. 75% of treatment sites dosed patients under expanded label.

2025 Guidance: Company maintained guidance of net product revenue between $2.9 billion and $3.1 billion, representing confidence in acceleration. Expected modest growth in Q3 followed by approximately double-digit growth in Q4 2024.

Financial Achievement: Company achieved cash flow positive status for the first time in company history, representing a major milestone.
